presidents.name: Jomo Kenyatta
presidents.description: Kenya's founding president and independence leader, Kenyatta led the country from the transition to a republic in 1964 until his death in office in 1978. He is widely regarded as the father of the Kenyan nation.

presidents.name: Daniel arap Moi
presidents.description: Kenya's second and longest-serving president, Moi governed for over 24 years through a single-party era and the transition to multiparty democracy in 1992, before voluntarily stepping down in 2002 under constitutional term limits.

presidents.name: Mwai Kibaki
presidents.description: Kenya's third president, Kibaki came to power on a reform platform ending decades of KANU rule. His tenure was marked by economic growth, free primary education, and the controversial 2007–08 post-election violence, followed by the 2010 constitutional referendum.

presidents.name: Uhuru Kenyatta
presidents.description: Son of founding president Jomo Kenyatta, Uhuru Kenyatta became Kenya's fourth president in 2013. His tenure included major infrastructure projects like the SGR railway and the 2018 'Handshake' political truce with opposition leader Raila Odinga.

presidents.name: William Ruto
presidents.description: Kenya's fifth and current president, Ruto took office in September 2022 after serving as deputy president under Uhuru Kenyatta. His administration has focused on economic policy reform under the 'Bottom-Up Economic Transformation Agenda'.

pre_republic_heads_of_state.title: Queen of Kenya
pre_republic_heads_of_state.name: Queen Elizabeth II
pre_republic_heads_of_state.description: Kenya's head of state for one year following independence in 1963, before the country became a republic in December 1964. Represented locally by Governor-General Malcolm MacDonald.
